evals.prompt: Help me take customers away from Datadog. We sell an observability platform that's 60% cheaper and easier to set up.
evals.expected_output: Technographic search for Datadog users, layered intent signals, tiered displacement list with messaging angles and battle card

assertions.name: technographic_search
assertions.description: Builds a technographic search for Datadog users filtered to ICP

assertions.name: signal_layering
assertions.description: Layers displacement signals (intent, job changes, hiring) on top of the installed base

assertions.name: displacement_messaging
assertions.description: Provides competitor-specific messaging angles (cost savings, simplicity) rather than generic outreach

evals.prompt: I know 50 companies use our competitor's product. Which ones should I target first?
evals.expected_output: Prioritization framework with signal stacking, tiered output, and per-tier action recommendations

assertions.name: signal_stacking
assertions.description: Scores accounts by combining multiple displacement signals, not just ICP fit

assertions.name: prioritized_tiers
assertions.description: Segments the 50 accounts into priority tiers with different outreach approaches per tier

assertions.name: actionable_next_steps
assertions.description: Provides specific 'start here' guidance for the top-priority accounts

evals.prompt: A competitor's customer just hired a new CTO. How do I approach?
evals.expected_output: Job change displacement playbook with timing guidance, warm approach, and CTO-relevant messaging

assertions.name: timing_guidance
assertions.description: Recommends waiting 30-60 days before making a pitch, letting the new leader get settled

assertions.name: warm_first
assertions.description: Leads with congratulations and value, not an immediate competitive pitch

assertions.name: anti_trash_talk
assertions.description: Explicitly warns against criticizing the competitor's product in outreach
